methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Pano AI is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Sonia Kastner with $89M total raised and a last post-money valuation of $500M+. The peer set comprises five same-sector, same-stage private companies—Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita Factory ($120M)—plus one public comparable, reflecting a dispersed valuation landscape in frontier climate technology.

Two methods were applied to triangulate fair value. The stage-progression method, using a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies $950M based on the $500M current valuation. The peer-median method, applied across the five same-stage, same-sector comparables, yields $310M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B, indicating material dispersion among peers. This wide range reflects heterogeneous risk profiles and technology maturity within the Climate & Energy sector at Series B.

Triangulating these two approaches produces a valuation range of $264M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $630M. The current $500M valuation sits in the lower-to-middle portion of this range. Key uncertainties center on whether Pano AI's technology maturity and market traction align more closely with Commonwealth Fusion Systems—a significant valuation outlier—or with the tighter $300M-$400M cluster, and the degree to which Series B climate venture valuations will sustain in a shifting capital environment.
